rulings.Case Title: FLOYD JONES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: CORNELL M. JANES et al VS. A. W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Under Ccp Section 473 To Vacate Order Granting Defendant Crane Co.S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 28, 2008, line 2. PLAINTIFFS CORNELL JANES, SUSAN JANES, SUSAN JANES AND PEGGY DEAN'S Motion Under CCP Section 473 To Vacate Order Granting Defendant Crane Co's Motion For Summary Judgment is denied. Counsel involved in this case are experienced asbestos litigators. On both sides of the case, counsel regularly argue for strict compliance with the mandates of CCP section 437c and motions often turn on literal compliance with the procedural requirements of CCP section 437c. This case is an example. Plaintiff's counsel's failure to seek a timely continuance is unfortunate for the clients. But if this excuse were accepted, it would undermine the constraints of CCP section 437c. The Court also finds the holding in English v. IKON Business Solutions, Inc. (2001) 94 Cal. App. 4th 130, 138 persuasive. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: PETER GALASSI et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Under Ccp Sectio 473 To Vacate Order Granting Defendant Crane Co.S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 28, 2008, line 3. PLAINTIFFS PETER GALASSI, LOUISE GALASSI, DEANNE HILBURN AND DEBRA GALASSI'S Motion Under CCP Section 473 To Vacate Order Granting Defendant Crane Co's Motion For Summary Judgment is denied. Counsel involved in this case are experienced asbestos litigators. On both sides of the case, counsel regularly argue for strict compliance with the mandates of CCP section 437c and motions often turn on literal compliance with the procedural requirements of CCP section 437c. This case is an example. Plaintiff's counsel's failure to seek a timely continuance is unfortunate for the clients. But if this excuse were accepted, it would undermine the constraints of CCP section 437c. The Court also finds the holding in English v. IKON Business Solutions, Inc. (2001) 94 Cal. App. 4th 130, 138 persuasive. The basis for the Court's ruling granting summary judgment is the failure of counsel to seek a continuance prior to the due date for the opposition. As a consequence, the opposition was not timely filed. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SHERIDAN CONCEPTS & CONSTRUCTION VS. HUGH MEAKIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Remove Or Reduce Mechanic'S Lien
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 28, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANTS HUGH MEAKIN, MELBA MEAKIN, SARAH MEAKIN AND PHILLIP MEAKIN'S Motion For An Order To Remove Or Reduce Mechanic's Lien is denied. The decision in Kim v. JF Enterprises (1996) 42 Cal. App. 4th 849, 856 establishes that knowledge of a contractor's work provides a basis to estop the owner from relying on the notice requirements of CC section 3097. The declarations in this case raise a question as to the existence of a contract between plaintiff and defendants, but establish that Hugh Meakin had knowledge of the work. Defendants have failed to provide sufficient evidence to reduce the amount of the lien. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ALEKSANDRA A VOLKOV VS. RECONSTRUCT COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 28, 2008, line 12. DEFENDANT MORTAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEM INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained. A hearing is required to determine whether plaintiff may amend. The notice of default is alleged to have been given by Reconstruct on April 25, 2008. It is also alleged that Reconstruct was not substituted as trustee until July 31, 2008. That being so, is the notice of default effective? May plaintiff rely on Civil Code section 2943(b)(2) in seeking a beneficiary statement? If plaintiff has not received a beneficiary statement, how is that plaintiff may be required to tender the full amount of the loan? Plaintiff may not rely on Civil Code sections 2943.5 and 2924.8 because they did not go into effect when the trustee sale was set unless the notice of the trustee sale was not effective given the notice being filed by Reconstructive before notice that it was trustee. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ALEX D. PATEK VS. RECONSTRUCT COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 28, 2008, line 13. DEFENDANT MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEM, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The demurrer is sustained. A hearing is required to determine whether plaintiff may amend. The notice of default is alleged to have been given by Reconstruct on May 9, 2008. It is also alleged that Reconstruct was not substituted as trustee until August 14, 2008. That being so, is the notice of default effective? May plaintiff rely on Civil Code section 2943(b)(2) in seeking a beneficiary statement? If plaintiff has not received a beneficiary statement, how is that plaintiff may be required to tender the full amount of the loan? Plaintiff may not rely on Civil Code sections 2943.5 and 2924.8 because they did not go into effect when the trustee sale was set unless the notice of the trustee sale was not effective given the notice being filed by Reconstructive before notice that it was trustee. =(302/PJM/PB)
